incident_summary: Two raw material lots LOT-23137 and LOT-79045 mixed during dispensing for batch B1193C — both materials look identical. Mix-up detected by second operator during verification before batch manufacture began.
root_cause_detail: Both lots stored adjacent to each other on same shelf. Labels had similar colouring. Operator picked from wrong position under dim lighting conditions in dispensary.
corrective_action: Mixed quantities discarded. New quantities dispensed from correct identified lots. Dispensary lighting levels measured and found below recommended 500 lux — emergency lighting upgrade initiated.
preventive_action: Dispensary storage reorganised — similar-looking materials stored in separate zones with physical dividers. Lighting upgraded to 750 lux. Barcode scan of material and lot mandatory before dispensing.
effectiveness_check: Zero material mix-up events for 12 months. Dispensary lighting compliance ≥500 lux on quarterly environmental check.

incident_summary: Coating pan LAF-910 experienced nozzle blockage during batch A9886C film coating. Spray rate dropped causing uneven coat weight — 25.5% of tablets outside ±7.3% uniformity limit.
root_cause_detail: Nozzle orifice blocked by dried coating solution due to inadequate purging at end of previous run.
corrective_action: Coating halted. Nozzle removed, ultrasonically cleaned, and flow rate verified. Batch quarantined and reworked after QA approval. Equipment FP-242 re-validated before return to service.
preventive_action: Nozzle purge step added to start-up and end-of-run checklist. Nozzle inspection added to weekly PM schedule. Coating solution viscosity specification tightened with incoming test mandatory.
effectiveness_check: Zero coating OOS events over next 10 batches on this equipment. Nozzle PM compliance ≥ 100% for 3 months.

incident_summary: Label batch LOT-81252 from PharmChem Ltd — barcode scan failure rate 5.5% on incoming inspection. Required scan success rate: ≥99.9%. Labels unreadable by automated dispensing and packing systems.
root_cause_detail: Supplier printer head alignment had drifted — narrow bars in Code 128 barcode below minimum width. Defect only apparent at scan, not visible inspection. Supplier had no outgoing scan verification.
corrective_action: Lot rejected. Emergency reprint commissioned at alternative print supplier with 48-hour turnaround. Incoming 100% barcode scan check performed on emergency lot — passed.
preventive_action: 100% barcode scan verification required from supplier with each label delivery — scan report attached to COA. Incoming 100% scan added to label QC procedure.
effectiveness_check: Zero barcode scan failures on incoming label lots for 24 months. 100% supplier scan certificate compliance on monthly QC log.

incident_summary: Tablet press FP-800 punch wear resulted in weight variation OOS during compression of batch D2841B. RSD 3.7% against limit 2%. 8% of tablets outside ±5% weight limit.
root_cause_detail: Punches worn beyond allowable limit. No intermediate wear measurement performed between batches. Wear only detected at batch failure.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Worn punches replaced. Batch 100% weight-sorted; OOS tablets rejected. Replacement punches qualified before restart.
preventive_action: Punch wear gauge check added to start-up procedure and every 2 hours mid-run. Punch replacement triggered at 80% of maximum wear limit.
effectiveness_check: Zero punch-wear-related OOS events on this press for 12 months. Punch inspection records reviewed on monthly QA audit.

corrective_action: All HVAC return air filters replaced immediately. Enhanced sanitisation of Grade C corridor performed. Trend analysis reviewed by QA — no action limit breaches — batch acceptable.
preventive_action: Critical HVAC spare parts maintained in minimum stock level — reorder triggered at 3 units. PM for HVAC filters cannot be delayed beyond 7 days without QA Director approval.

corrective_action: Duct inspected, cleaned, and all retained material weighed and documented. Batch yield reconciled. Dust extraction system added to between-batch cleaning procedure immediately.
preventive_action: Dust extraction duct cleaning made mandatory between batches — documented in line clearance checklist. Yield alert threshold set at 97% — QA review triggered automatically below this level.
effectiveness_check: Yield consistently ≥95% for 12 months. Zero unaccounted material loss events on monthly yield trend review.

incident_summary: HVAC system failure in Grade B manufacturing suite C — differential pressure dropped to 7 Pa (limit ≥15 Pa) for 5.9 hours during batch B8801B.

incident_summary: Superseded SOP SOP-004 v3.7 used during batch A7380A manufacture. Current approved version is v7.0. Paper copy not removed from area when new version issued.

incident_summary: In-process pH of batch D5840A measured 3.7 — below validated range lower limit of 5.5 during granulation. Batch held pending investigation.

corrective_action: Batch rejected. Water system investigated — vent filter replaced and water re-qualified. pH specification for WFI tightened. Batch manufacturing process fully reviewed.
preventive_action: In-line pH monitoring of WFI at point of use — alert at pH 5.8, process stop at pH 5.5. Vent filter change now triggers WFI re-qualification before manufacturing use.
effectiveness_check: WFI pH within specification for 12 months on daily monitoring log. Zero pH-related granulation failures.

incident_summary: Balance HP-892 failed calibration check before dispensing for batch D9014C. Drift of 0.26g recorded — tolerance ±0.01g. Previous calibration 5 weeks prior — due date missed.
root_cause_detail: Calibration due date not tracked in equipment management system. Balance due dates maintained manually in a spreadsheet — entry not updated after last calibration.
corrective_action: Balance taken out of service. All quantities dispensed for batch re-weighed on calibrated balance. No weight discrepancies found — batch released.
preventive_action: All balance calibration due dates entered into LIMS with auto-alert 7 days before due. No dispensing activity permitted on balance with overdue calibration.
effectiveness_check: 100% on-time calibration for all balances for 12 months on monthly compliance report.

incident_summary: Sterility failure for batch D7531B — Staphylococcus epidermidis detected in 2/14 samples. Batch D4587B (3606 units) quarantined.
root_cause_detail: Gowning breach identified via CCTV review — operator touched non-sterile surface and continued filling without re-gloving. No supervisor present in fill suite at time of breach.

preventive_action: Continuous CCTV monitoring of fill suite with real-time QA review station. Supervisor presence in fill suite mandatory for first 30 minutes of each fill campaign. Re-gloving stations installed at 3 fill positions.

incident_summary: Temperature excursion in cold room CR-06 — reached 10.2°C for 3.7 hours. Limit 2–8°C. Batch C6778C and 2 other product batches potentially affected.
root_cause_detail: Refrigeration compressor failed overnight. Night shift operator did not respond to alarm within required 15 minutes — response was {round(random.uniform(45,120),0)} minutes after initial alarm.
corrective_action: Compressor replaced. All affected batches quarantined and thermal stability assessments initiated per product stability data. Engineering root cause investigation completed.
preventive_action: 24/7 alarm escalation: unacknowledged alarm after 10 minutes auto-escalates to on-call engineer via SMS. Backup refrigeration unit installed in critical cold rooms. Alarm response training refreshed for all shifts.

incident_summary: Incorrect cleaning agent (Quaternary Ammonium) applied to equipment FP-113 by operator OP-1591 after batch A9627A manufacture. Approved agent is Peracetic Acid 0.2%.

corrective_action: Equipment re-cleaned with correct approved agent. Residue swab tested — passed before next manufacturing use. Incorrect agent containers removed from area immediately.
preventive_action: Cleaning agents dispensed from locked cabinet — key released only after correct agent confirmed on cleaning record. Containers colour-coded per equipment type. Cleaning SOP updated with photo guide.
effectiveness_check: Zero incorrect cleaning agent events for 12 months. Monthly cleaning audit: 100% correct agent usage verified.

corrective_action: Area evacuated. Batch risk-assessed — manufacturing in early stage, low risk. Batch accepted with documented QA justification. HVAC repaired with new belt and alignment check.
preventive_action: Redundant HVAC fan installed in Grade B suite. HVAC alarms mirrored to production area panel and QA office. Response procedure added to area emergency response document.
effectiveness_check: Zero HVAC differential pressure failures for 12 months. Alarm response time ≤15 minutes on monthly alarm log.

incident_summary: Laboratory analyst OP-2656 performed dissolution testing of batch D9398C without completing analytical method transfer training for this product. Results invalid.

corrective_action: Results invalidated. Analyst {op()} trained and assessed on dissolution method. Test repeated by qualified analyst. Batch released after confirmatory results.
preventive_action: Product transfer checklist includes mandatory training plan update as step 1 — no analytical work permitted on transferred product until training plan confirmed current by QA.
effectiveness_check: Zero unqualified analyst testing events for 12 months. 100% product transfer training plan completion before first batch tested.

incident_summary: API lot LOT-66811 from PharmChem Ltd failed assay — 95.2% against specification of 98.0–102.0%. Three retests confirmed OOS. Certificate of Analysis stated 99.7%.
root_cause_detail: Supplier analytical method was not equivalent to Ph. Eur. pharmacopoeial method used by the pharmaceutical manufacturer. Supplier method overestimated purity due to unresolved impurity peak.
corrective_action: Lot rejected and returned to supplier. Emergency stock from alternative qualified supplier sourced. Formal investigation requested from supplier within 30 days.
preventive_action: API supplier method comparability study mandated for all new lots — correlation between supplier and in-house methods must be ≥99% before lot acceptance. Annual method comparability audit introduced.
effectiveness_check: Zero API assay OOS results from supplier method discrepancy for 24 months. Annual method comparability confirmed on supplier audit report.

incident_summary: Wrong batch number entered in electronic batch record for batch C9217C by operator OP-1749. Error discovered during QA batch record review — two batches had same number in system.
root_cause_detail: Operator manually typed batch number rather than scanning barcode from manufacturing order. Keyboard transposition created duplicate batch number. EBR did not flag the duplicate.
corrective_action: Batch record corrected with full audit trail. Investigation confirmed no product mix-up occurred. System vendor engaged to add duplicate batch number validation.
preventive_action: EBR updated to reject duplicate batch numbers at entry — system alert raised immediately. Batch number entry by keyboard disabled — scan only via barcode reader.
effectiveness_check: Zero duplicate batch number events in EBR for 12 months.

incident_summary: Tablet press GR-713 punch wear resulted in weight variation OOS during compression of batch C2591A. RSD 3.9% against limit 2%. 15% of tablets outside ±5% weight limit.
root_cause_detail: Punches worn beyond allowable limit. No intermediate wear measurement performed between batches. Wear only detected at batch failure.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Worn punches replaced. Batch 100% weight-sorted; OOS tablets rejected. Replacement punches qualified before restart.
preventive_action: Punch wear gauge check added to start-up procedure and every 2 hours mid-run. Punch replacement triggered at 80% of maximum wear limit.
effectiveness_check: Zero punch-wear-related OOS events on this press for 12 months. Punch inspection records reviewed on monthly QA audit.

incident_summary: Water purification system BLD-454 conductivity exceeded 3.1 µS/cm — WFI specification limit 1.3 µS/cm. Batch B8568C manufacturing in progress at time of exceedance.
root_cause_detail: Resin bed in deionisation stage exhausted earlier than predicted due to increased daily water demand from new process line.
corrective_action: WFI system taken offline. Resin replaced and system re-validated. Batch quarantined and risk-assessed — manufacturing steps using affected WFI identified.
preventive_action: WFI conductivity monitored online with automated batch-stop alert. Resin replacement schedule revised based on actual usage, not fixed interval.
effectiveness_check: WFI conductivity within specification for 12 consecutive months. Zero batches manufactured with out-of-specification WFI.

incident_summary: Ambient temperature in dispensary A rose to 28.7°C for 7.9 hours due to air conditioning failure. Temperature-sensitive materials for batch C5981B dispensed during exceedance.
root_cause_detail: AC unit capacitor failed — no alarm configured for dispensary temperature. Exceedance discovered during routine operator check {round(random.uniform(2,5),1)} hours after onset.
corrective_action: Dispensed materials quarantined and tested for conformance against stability data. AC unit repaired. Materials released after QA review of stability data.
preventive_action: Continuous temperature monitoring with alarm to QA office and engineering installed in all dispensary areas. AC unit added to monthly PM schedule. Backup portable AC unit available for emergency deployment.
effectiveness_check: Dispensary temperature within 15–25°C for 12 months on continuous monitoring. Zero undetected temperature exceedances.

incident_summary: Foreign particulate contamination found in packaging material lot LOT-82933 — metal fragments found in 23.6% of units during incoming inspection. Fragments identified as stainless steel filings.
root_cause_detail: Supplier equipment maintenance performed during production run — metal swarf from drilling operation entered open packaging material containers before cleaning completed.
corrective_action: Lot rejected. 100% metal detector inspection of all packaging materials from this supplier in stock. Clear — no other lots affected. Supplier placed on probationary status.
preventive_action: Metal detector check added to all incoming packaging material inspection — no materials accepted without metal detection certificate from supplier. Annual unannounced supplier audit initiated.
effectiveness_check: Zero particulate contamination events from packaging materials for 24 months. Annual supplier audit report confirms maintenance controls adequate.

incident_summary: Two raw material lots LOT-74858 and LOT-70806 mixed during dispensing for batch C8363A — both materials look identical. Mix-up detected by second operator during verification before batch manufacture began.
root_cause_detail: Both lots stored adjacent to each other on same shelf. Labels had similar colouring. Operator picked from wrong position under dim lighting conditions in dispensary.
corrective_action: Mixed quantities discarded. New quantities dispensed from correct identified lots. Dispensary lighting levels measured and found below recommended 500 lux — emergency lighting upgrade initiated.
preventive_action: Dispensary storage reorganised — similar-looking materials stored in separate zones with physical dividers. Lighting upgraded to 750 lux. Barcode scan of material and lot mandatory before dispensing.
effectiveness_check: Zero material mix-up events for 12 months. Dispensary lighting compliance ≥500 lux on quarterly environmental check.

root_cause_detail: Supervisor was unaware that the 4-hour reporting requirement had been updated in SOP {sop()} 6 weeks prior. Training on the update was not completed — supervisor name absent from training completion list.
corrective_action: Late deviation accepted with documented justification. Supervisor {op()} trained on updated SOP immediately. All supervisors verified against training completion list for this SOP.
preventive_action: SOP updates with regulatory impact (including reporting timelines) require supervisor sign-off within 5 working days of issue. QMS blocks batch release if supervisor training not current.
effectiveness_check: 100% deviation reporting within required timeframe for 12 months on monthly deviation metrics report.

root_cause_detail: Document control procedure requires area managers to retrieve superseded SOPs on version change. Area manager on leave — retrieval task not delegated. Superseded copy remained accessible for {days()} days.

incident_summary: Balance FD-581 failed calibration check before dispensing for batch D2152B. Drift of 0.48g recorded — tolerance ±0.01g. Previous calibration 5 weeks prior — due date missed.
root_cause_detail: Calibration due date not tracked in equipment management system. Balance due dates maintained manually in a spreadsheet — entry not updated after last calibration.
corrective_action: Balance taken out of service. All quantities dispensed for batch re-weighed on calibrated balance. No weight discrepancies found — batch released.
preventive_action: All balance calibration due dates entered into LIMS with auto-alert 7 days before due. No dispensing activity permitted on balance with overdue calibration.
effectiveness_check: 100% on-time calibration for all balances for 12 months on monthly compliance report.

incident_summary: Analyst OP-6942 prepared HPLC standard solution at 81.0% of required concentration for batch C3593C assay. Results reported before error identified by second analyst during peer review.
root_cause_detail: Analyst misread SOP — volumetric flask size specified was 100mL but analyst used 250mL. Calculation error not identified before solution use. Peer review is advisory, not mandatory.
corrective_action: All results for batch invalidated. Standard solution remade at correct concentration. Analysis repeated by senior analyst. Correct results obtained — batch released.
preventive_action: Mandatory second-analyst verification of standard solution preparation before analysis begins. LIMS calculation check added — system flags if standard response factor deviates >5% from expected.
effectiveness_check: Zero standard preparation errors for 12 months. 100% peer review compliance on monthly QC audit.

incident_summary: Incorrect cleaning agent (IPA 60%) applied to equipment FP-697 by operator OP-5963 after batch B1197B manufacture. Approved agent is Peracetic Acid 0.2%.
root_cause_detail: Cleaning agents stored side-by-side in unlabelled containers on cleaning trolley. Operator selected wrong bottle. Cleaning agent inventory not controlled at point of use.
corrective_action: Equipment re-cleaned with correct approved agent. Residue swab tested — passed before next manufacturing use. Incorrect agent containers removed from area immediately.
preventive_action: Cleaning agents dispensed from locked cabinet — key released only after correct agent confirmed on cleaning record. Containers colour-coded per equipment type. Cleaning SOP updated with photo guide.
effectiveness_check: Zero incorrect cleaning agent events for 12 months. Monthly cleaning audit: 100% correct agent usage verified.

incident_summary: Sterility failure for batch B9605B — Staphylococcus epidermidis detected in 3/17 samples. Batch D1233C (4878 units) quarantined.
root_cause_detail: Gowning breach identified via CCTV review — operator touched non-sterile surface and continued filling without re-gloving. No supervisor present in fill suite at time of breach.
corrective_action: Batch recalled and destroyed. Filling line decontaminated and re-qualified. Operator suspended pending investigation. Supervisor accountability review initiated.
preventive_action: Continuous CCTV monitoring of fill suite with real-time QA review station. Supervisor presence in fill suite mandatory for first 30 minutes of each fill campaign. Re-gloving stations installed at 3 fill positions.
effectiveness_check: Zero sterility failures for 18 months. 100% gowning compliance on weekly CCTV audit.

incident_summary: Blend uniformity failure for batch B4318B — RSD 7.3% against limit 3%. Blending time 14.0 minutes against validated 28 minutes due to blender availability issue.
root_cause_detail: Production scheduling allocated insufficient blend time due to equipment conflict with another batch. Validation parameter deviation not escalated to QA before manufacture.
corrective_action: Batch rejected. Blend uniformity testing completed on retained samples to confirm extent of failure. Blender scheduling reviewed and corrected for all upcoming batches.
preventive_action: Critical process parameters (including blend time) flagged in EBR — system alerts QA if scheduled time deviates from validated range before batch release to manufacture.
effectiveness_check: Zero blend uniformity OOS events for 12 months. 100% validated blend time compliance on monthly process review.

incident_summary: Dissolution failure at 60 minute timepoint for batch A6224C — 51.0% dissolved against NLT 82%. Three consecutive batches failed same timepoint.
root_cause_detail: Disintegrant particle size from new supplier lot was outside historical range. Smaller particle size reduced disintegrant performance. Incoming test for particle size was not performed on this excipient.
corrective_action: Three batches rejected. Emergency particle size testing of all remaining disintegrant stock. Alternative approved supplier lot sourced. Reformulation study initiated.
preventive_action: Particle size testing added to incoming QC specification for disintegrant. Supplier change notification procedure tightened — any new supplier lot requires comparative dissolution testing before use.
effectiveness_check: Zero dissolution failures for 12 months. 100% incoming particle size compliance for disintegrant on monthly QC report.

incident_summary: Batch record for batch D4191C missing authorised signature at critical step 20 — fill start. Identified during QA batch record review before release.
root_cause_detail: Operator completed the step but was called away urgently before signing. Paper batch record was not locked — a different operator moved it, and the unsigned step was missed during QA review.
corrective_action: Batch record corrected with retrospective signature and deviation report. Investigation confirmed step was performed correctly. Batch released after QA Director review.
preventive_action: EBR system deployed for this product — paper batch records eliminated. EBR locks step until operator signs electronically in real time. Step cannot be bypassed.
effectiveness_check: Zero missing signatures in EBR for 12 months. 100% step sign-off compliance on monthly EBR audit.

incident_summary: QC analyst OP-5328 applied incorrect sampling plan to Sodium Starch Glycolate lot LOT-79755 incoming inspection — used Tier 2 plan for Tier 1 supplier. Under-sampled — missed non-conforming portion of lot.
root_cause_detail: Supplier tier assignment changed 2 months prior. Training on updated sampling procedure {sop()} had not been cascaded to all QC analysts — only 3 of 8 analysts had completed training.
corrective_action: Full re-sampling of lot performed using correct Tier 1 plan. Non-conforming portion identified and rejected. Remaining lot accepted after full testing. Training gap for all 8 analysts remediated immediately.
preventive_action: SOP updates triggering any change to sampling plans require mandatory training completion before effective date. SOP change cannot go live until training manager confirms 100% affected-staff completion.
effectiveness_check: Zero incorrect sampling plan application for 12 months. 100% SOP training completion before effective date on monthly training compliance report.

incident_summary: QA analyst OP-9122 approved batch record for batch B3929A with 3 incomplete critical steps. Error discovered during post-release annual product review. Batch had been distributed.
root_cause_detail: QA reviewer working under time pressure at end of shift. Batch record review checklist used was outdated — 3 new mandatory steps added 2 months prior not on old checklist.
corrective_action: Batch recalled from distribution for risk assessment. Retrospective review of incomplete steps — all found to have been performed; documentation gap only. Batch rereleased after QA Director review.
preventive_action: QA batch record review checklist version-controlled in LIMS — old versions automatically retired on system when new version approved. Checklist update triggers mandatory retraining notification.
effectiveness_check: Zero incomplete batch record releases for 12 months. Monthly QA batch record audit: 100% checklist compliance.

incident_summary: Operator OP-6243 initiated aseptic filling of batch D2686C without completing gowning qualification check. Filling proceeded for 3.1 hours before QA supervisor identified incomplete gown verification.
root_cause_detail: Gowning sign-off log was physically in the change room but operator was not aware that entry to fill suite requires documented sign-off. New operator — training not fully completed.
corrective_action: Filling halted. Batch quarantined and sterility risk-assessed. Operator removed from aseptic operations pending requalification. Gowning sign-off retrospectively reviewed.
preventive_action: Electronic gowning sign-off integrated with fill suite door access system — door does not release unless gowning record complete. New operators may not enter Grade A suite unaccompanied for first 3 months.
effectiveness_check: Zero unauthorised Grade A entries for 12 months. Gowning compliance 100% on weekly CCTV audit.

incident_summary: Tablet hardness for batch D1092B failed specification — 61.0% of tablets below minimum 7.2 kP during IPC at 75% compression.

corrective_action: Compression halted. Bearing replaced. Batch 100% checked by automated hardness tester. Sub-specification tablets rejected. Remaining batch released after QA review.
preventive_action: Compression force monitoring added to real-time SCADA display with alert at ±5% from setpoint. Main compression roll bearing added to 6-monthly PM schedule.
effectiveness_check: Zero hardness OOS events on this press for 12 months. Bearing inspection records reviewed quarterly.

incident_summary: Moisture ingress in Sodium Starch Glycolate lot LOT-18913 — 7.5% moisture against NMT 2.0%. Packaging appeared intact. Discovered during incoming QC testing.
root_cause_detail: Supplier had temporary storage issue — materials stored in non-climate-controlled area during transit delay. Packaging moisture barrier integrity not tested before shipment.
corrective_action: Lot rejected. All in-house stock from same supplier lot quarantined and tested. {random.randint(2,5)} additional lots found non-compliant — all rejected.
preventive_action: Moisture indicator card added inside all excipient shipments — colour change on receipt triggers automatic rejection. Storage condition declaration required from supplier for all shipments.
effectiveness_check: Zero moisture OOS results on incoming testing for 12 months. 100% moisture indicator check compliance on incoming QC log.

incident_summary: Stability sample for batch D5630C not collected at 9-month timepoint. Missing collection discovered 86 days after due date during stability manager review.
root_cause_detail: Stability schedule transferred manually to QC calendar on product changeover. Transfer error omitted this specific batch. No automated reminder system in place.
corrective_action: Accelerated stability assessment performed on retained samples. Bracketing and matrixing approach justified the missing timepoint per ICH Q1D. Regulatory authority notified per reporting requirements.
preventive_action: Stability schedules auto-generated and uploaded to LIMS on batch release. QC analyst receives LIMS reminder 7 and 3 days before each pull date. Missed timepoints auto-escalate to QA within 24 hours of due date.
effectiveness_check: Zero missed stability timepoints for 24 months. Monthly LIMS stability compliance report reviewed at QA management meeting.

incident_summary: Relative humidity in tablet manufacturing suite rose to 75.0% — limit ≤55% RH. Exceedance lasted 6.4 hours during batch D5536B compression. Moisture-sensitive product at risk.
root_cause_detail: Dehumidification unit failed during seasonal high-humidity period. Preventive maintenance had not identified developing fault — unit operating at reduced capacity before failure.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Batch moisture content tested on retained samples — passed. Dehumidification unit repaired. Room re-qualified before restart.
preventive_action: Humidity alert set at 50% RH — dehumidification increases automatically before limit reached. Dehumidification unit performance check added to monthly PM including capacity verification.
effectiveness_check: RH within 55% limit for 12 months on continuous monitoring log. Zero humidity-related process holds.

incident_summary: Particle size of Microcrystalline Cellulose lot LOT-62620 from ExciPure Corp outside specification — D90 288.0µm against NMT 200µm. Discovered during incoming QC. Previous lots all compliant.
root_cause_detail: Supplier milling equipment calibration had drifted — milling screen replaced without re-qualification of particle size output. Change not notified to pharmaceutical customer.
corrective_action: Lot rejected. Supplier formally notified. All batches manufactured with this lot quarantined — dissolution testing performed. {random.randint(2,4)} batches showed dissolution impact and were rejected.
preventive_action: Supplier quality agreement updated — any equipment change affecting particle size requires 30-day prior notification and sample submission for qualification testing before shipment.
effectiveness_check: Zero particle size OOS results from this supplier for 24 months. Annual supplier process capability review for critical quality attributes.

incident_summary: Visible particulate found in 60 vials during 100% visual inspection of batch B4094A (4688 total vials). Particles identified as glass lamellae from vial inner surface.
root_cause_detail: Glass vial supplier had process change — new vial mould introduced without notifying pharmaceutical customer. New mould produced higher rate of glass delamination under washing/sterilisation conditions.
corrective_action: Batch rejected and destroyed. All vials from same supplier lot quarantined. Supplier formally notified. Emergency supply sourced from qualified alternative supplier for ongoing production.
preventive_action: Supplier change notification clause added to vial supplier quality agreement — any process change requires prior notification and sample submission for qualification. Incoming vial delamination test introduced.
effectiveness_check: Zero glass particulate events for 12 months. Annual vial supplier audit to verify change notification process.

incident_summary: EBR system data loss for batch B5963A step 16 due to server timeout during peak usage. Operator entry not saved — discovered when operator logged off.
root_cause_detail: Server infrastructure had insufficient capacity for concurrent users. Timeout setting was 8 minutes — insufficient for complex data entry steps. No auto-save feature implemented.
corrective_action: Missing data recovered from server transaction log backup. Data re-entered with audit trail explanation. Server capacity upgraded with emergency budget approval.
preventive_action: EBR auto-save implemented every 90 seconds. Server capacity upgraded to handle 150% of peak concurrent user load. Session timeout extended to 45 minutes with 5-minute warning.
effectiveness_check: Zero EBR data loss events for 12 months. Server uptime ≥99.9% on monthly IT report.

incident_summary: Autoclave CEN-150 temperature sensor drifted — batch D8185B sterilisation load recorded 118.4°C against required 121°C minimum. Biological indicator result pending.
root_cause_detail: Thermocouple calibration drift identified. Calibration due date was 3 weeks prior — PM task delayed due to production scheduling conflict.
corrective_action: Load quarantined. Thermocouple replaced and calibrated. Biological indicator result: growth detected — load re-sterilised. Batch risk-assessed by QA.
preventive_action: Autoclave calibration scheduled independently of production — engineering calendar separated from production planning. Calibration overdue = automatic production hold on that autoclave.
effectiveness_check: 100% on-time calibration for autoclave sensors for 12 months. Zero sterilisation failures attributed to sensor drift.

incident_summary: Granule moisture content 6.8% exceeded validated limit of 3.5% for batch B3975B. Drying endpoint not met — drying step terminated early by operator.
root_cause_detail: Operator terminated drying at fixed time rather than verifying moisture content as required by SOP. Drying endpoint is moisture-based, not time-based per validated procedure.
corrective_action: Additional drying cycle performed. Moisture re-tested and passed at 2.8%. Batch released after QA review. Operator retrained on drying endpoint determination.
preventive_action: EBR updated — drying step cannot be marked complete until moisture content result entered and confirmed within specification. Fixed-time drying not permitted.

incident_summary: Foreign particulate contamination found in packaging material lot LOT-13735 — metal fragments found in 27.2% of units during incoming inspection. Fragments identified as stainless steel filings.

preventive_action: Metal detector check added to all incoming packaging material inspection — no materials accepted without metal detection certificate from supplier. Annual unannounced supplier audit initiated.

incident_summary: Two raw material lots LOT-18693 and LOT-24733 mixed during dispensing for batch D8609B — both materials look identical. Mix-up detected by second operator during verification before batch manufacture began.
root_cause_detail: Both lots stored adjacent to each other on same shelf. Labels had similar colouring. Operator picked from wrong position under dim lighting conditions in dispensary.
corrective_action: Mixed quantities discarded. New quantities dispensed from correct identified lots. Dispensary lighting levels measured and found below recommended 500 lux — emergency lighting upgrade initiated.
preventive_action: Dispensary storage reorganised — similar-looking materials stored in separate zones with physical dividers. Lighting upgraded to 750 lux. Barcode scan of material and lot mandatory before dispensing.

incident_summary: Tablet press FP-121 punch wear resulted in weight variation OOS during compression of batch D8191B. RSD 5.2% against limit 2%. 23% of tablets outside ±5% weight limit.

incident_summary: Stability sample for batch D7065B not collected at 6-month timepoint. Missing collection discovered 45 days after due date during stability manager review.

corrective_action: Accelerated stability assessment performed on retained samples. Bracketing and matrixing approach justified the missing timepoint per ICH Q1D. Regulatory authority notified per reporting requirements.
preventive_action: Stability schedules auto-generated and uploaded to LIMS on batch release. QC analyst receives LIMS reminder 7 and 3 days before each pull date. Missed timepoints auto-escalate to QA within 24 hours of due date.
effectiveness_check: Zero missed stability timepoints for 24 months. Monthly LIMS stability compliance report reviewed at QA management meeting.

incident_summary: In-process pH of batch A4732C measured 3.8 — below validated range lower limit of 5.4 during granulation. Batch held pending investigation.
root_cause_detail: Purified water used for granulation fluid had transient low pH of 5.5 due to CO2 absorption from environmental exposure after storage tank vent filter change.
corrective_action: Batch rejected. Water system investigated — vent filter replaced and water re-qualified. pH specification for WFI tightened. Batch manufacturing process fully reviewed.
preventive_action: In-line pH monitoring of WFI at point of use — alert at pH 5.8, process stop at pH 5.5. Vent filter change now triggers WFI re-qualification before manufacturing use.
effectiveness_check: WFI pH within specification for 12 months on daily monitoring log. Zero pH-related granulation failures.

corrective_action: Cycle aborted. PID settings restored and validated. Batch quarantined and assessed via appearance, moisture content, and reconstitution testing.

incident_summary: Granule moisture content 5.3% exceeded validated limit of 3.5% for batch A9251A. Drying endpoint not met — drying step terminated early by operator.
root_cause_detail: Operator terminated drying at fixed time rather than verifying moisture content as required by SOP. Drying endpoint is moisture-based, not time-based per validated procedure.
corrective_action: Additional drying cycle performed. Moisture re-tested and passed at 2.8%. Batch released after QA review. Operator retrained on drying endpoint determination.
preventive_action: EBR updated — drying step cannot be marked complete until moisture content result entered and confirmed within specification. Fixed-time drying not permitted.
effectiveness_check: Zero moisture OOS events at granulation for 12 months. 100% drying endpoint test compliance on monthly process audit.

incident_summary: LIMS system generated incorrect assay calculation for batch D7226A — formula error in assay calculation module introduced in version 7.1 upgrade.
root_cause_detail: Software upgrade validation did not include calculation verification against manual calculation for all test types. Dissolution module calculation tested for tablet dosage forms only — not vials.
corrective_action: All affected results manually recalculated. LIMS vendor provided emergency patch within 48 hours. Patch validated before installation. All results re-entered after patch confirmation.
preventive_action: LIMS upgrade validation protocol extended — all calculation modules must be verified against manual calculation for all dosage forms before go-live. No upgrade approved without QA sign-off on calculation check.
effectiveness_check: Zero LIMS calculation errors for 12 months. 100% upgrade validation compliance on change control audit.

incident_summary: HVAC system failure in Grade B manufacturing suite C — differential pressure dropped to 4 Pa (limit ≥15 Pa) for 1.4 hours during batch A7716C.
root_cause_detail: HVAC supply fan belt failed — no redundancy available on this system. Production was not notified of HVAC alarm for 45 minutes due to alarm panel location in engineering area.
corrective_action: Area evacuated. Batch risk-assessed — manufacturing in early stage, low risk. Batch accepted with documented QA justification. HVAC repaired with new belt and alignment check.
preventive_action: Redundant HVAC fan installed in Grade B suite. HVAC alarms mirrored to production area panel and QA office. Response procedure added to area emergency response document.
effectiveness_check: Zero HVAC differential pressure failures for 12 months. Alarm response time ≤15 minutes on monthly alarm log.

incident_summary: Blend uniformity failure for batch D2960A — RSD 7.8% against limit 3%. Blending time 10.4 minutes against validated 27 minutes due to blender availability issue.
root_cause_detail: Production scheduling allocated insufficient blend time due to equipment conflict with another batch. Validation parameter deviation not escalated to QA before manufacture.
corrective_action: Batch rejected. Blend uniformity testing completed on retained samples to confirm extent of failure. Blender scheduling reviewed and corrected for all upcoming batches.
preventive_action: Critical process parameters (including blend time) flagged in EBR — system alerts QA if scheduled time deviates from validated range before batch release to manufacture.
effectiveness_check: Zero blend uniformity OOS events for 12 months. 100% validated blend time compliance on monthly process review.

incident_summary: Yield loss of 12.1% recorded for batch D8850A against expected ≥95%. Material reconciliation gap of 4.7kg could not be accounted for in batch record.
root_cause_detail: Investigation found material retained in dust extraction ducting — duct had not been cleaned between batches due to production pressure. Material loss attributed to duct retention, not process loss.
corrective_action: Duct inspected, cleaned, and all retained material weighed and documented. Batch yield reconciled. Dust extraction system added to between-batch cleaning procedure immediately.
preventive_action: Dust extraction duct cleaning made mandatory between batches — documented in line clearance checklist. Yield alert threshold set at 97% — QA review triggered automatically below this level.
effectiveness_check: Yield consistently ≥95% for 12 months. Zero unaccounted material loss events on monthly yield trend review.

incident_summary: Superseded SOP SOP-006 v4.6 used during batch B7590A manufacture. Current approved version is v7.3. Paper copy not removed from area when new version issued.
root_cause_detail: Document control procedure requires area managers to retrieve superseded SOPs on version change. Area manager on leave — retrieval task not delegated. Superseded copy remained accessible for {days()} days.
corrective_action: Superseded SOP retrieved and destroyed. Batch risk-assessed — difference between versions was minor editorial change, no process impact. Batch released.
preventive_action: SOP issuance via document management system only — DMS locks access to superseded version at effective date. Area managers receive system alert to confirm receipt of new version before effective date.
effectiveness_check: Zero superseded SOP events for 12 months. Monthly DMS audit confirms all current SOPs accessed only from DMS.

incident_summary: Visible particulate found in 46 vials during 100% visual inspection of batch B4128C (4594 total vials). Particles identified as glass lamellae from vial inner surface.
root_cause_detail: Glass vial supplier had process change — new vial mould introduced without notifying pharmaceutical customer. New mould produced higher rate of glass delamination under washing/sterilisation conditions.
corrective_action: Batch rejected and destroyed. All vials from same supplier lot quarantined. Supplier formally notified. Emergency supply sourced from qualified alternative supplier for ongoing production.
preventive_action: Supplier change notification clause added to vial supplier quality agreement — any process change requires prior notification and sample submission for qualification. Incoming vial delamination test introduced.
effectiveness_check: Zero glass particulate events for 12 months. Annual vial supplier audit to verify change notification process.

incident_summary: HPLC instrument FD-767 column degradation produced OOS results for batch D5920C potency assay. Column pressure exceeded 511 bar during analysis run.
root_cause_detail: Column exceeded maximum injection count of 200. Column lifetime tracking not implemented — column replaced only on failure, not proactively.
corrective_action: Column replaced with new qualified column. All batch A4267A results invalidated and repeated using new column. System suitability confirmed before repeat analysis.
preventive_action: Column injection counter log implemented in LIMS. Mandatory replacement at 180 injections (90% of maximum). Column inventory managed with auto-alert at reorder point.
effectiveness_check: No column-related OOS events for 6 months post-implementation. 100% column injection count compliance on monthly audit.

incident_summary: Viable particle count in Grade B area exceeded action limit — 12 CFU/m³ at sample point 10 during start of batch D4240A campaign.
root_cause_detail: Drain in Grade B area identified as microbial reservoir — drain cover cracked allowing backflow during cleaning. Drain had not been inspected for {days()} days.
corrective_action: Drain cover replaced and drain sanitised with sporicidal agent. Enhanced cleaning protocol implemented. Area re-tested — all results within specification before restart.
preventive_action: Drain inspection added to weekly environmental walkthrough checklist. Trend analysis for Grade B EM results implemented — 2 consecutive alerts trigger immediate drain inspection.
effectiveness_check: Zero Grade B EM action limit exceedances for 12 months. Weekly drain inspection compliance 100%.

incident_summary: Grade A environmental monitoring exceeded action limit — 2 CFU detected at Fill Station #1 during batch A1864B. EU GMP Annex 1 limit: 0 CFU. Three consecutive positive samples.
root_cause_detail: HVAC pre-filter for LAF unit AC-420 overdue for replacement by 50 days. Filter bypass reduced airflow velocity below 0.45 m/s — unidirectional airflow protection compromised.
corrective_action: Fill area taken out of service. Full decontamination with sporicidal agent. Filter replaced. Grade A re-qualified over 3 consecutive days. Batch quarantined for sterility testing.
preventive_action: HVAC PM integrated with production scheduling system — both systems display same PM calendar. Automated alert to Engineering Lead 14 and 7 days before filter due date. LAF velocity check added to daily line clearance.
effectiveness_check: Zero Grade A EM exceedances for 18 months. LAF velocity within specification on all daily checks.

incident_summary: Wrong batch number entered in electronic batch record for batch C5968A by operator OP-3618. Error discovered during QA batch record review — two batches had same number in system.

corrective_action: Batch record corrected with full audit trail. Investigation confirmed no product mix-up occurred. System vendor engaged to add duplicate batch number validation.

incident_summary: Coating pan TBL-489 experienced nozzle blockage during batch B8702B film coating. Spray rate dropped causing uneven coat weight — 14.7% of tablets outside ±6.5% uniformity limit.

effectiveness_check: Zero coating OOS events over next 10 batches on this equipment. Nozzle PM compliance ≥ 100% for 3 months.

incident_summary: Relative humidity in tablet manufacturing suite rose to 76.0% — limit ≤55% RH. Exceedance lasted 7.0 hours during batch A1220C compression. Moisture-sensitive product at risk.
root_cause_detail: Dehumidification unit failed during seasonal high-humidity period. Preventive maintenance had not identified developing fault — unit operating at reduced capacity before failure.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Batch moisture content tested on retained samples — passed. Dehumidification unit repaired. Room re-qualified before restart.
preventive_action: Humidity alert set at 50% RH — dehumidification increases automatically before limit reached. Dehumidification unit performance check added to monthly PM including capacity verification.

incident_summary: Filling pump FP-863 o-ring failure caused variable fill volume on vial filling line 1 during batch B2502C. Fill weights ranged 0.92mL to 1.08mL against target 1.00mL ± 2%.
root_cause_detail: O-ring reached end of service life. PM interval of 6 months was insufficient for this filling speed. O-ring wear not inspected between PM events.
corrective_action: Filling halted. O-ring replaced. 100% gravimetric IPC check repeated across batch. Batch accepted after QA review of all fill weight data.
preventive_action: PM interval reduced from 6 to 3 months for all filling pump o-rings. O-ring inspection added to daily line clearance. Spare o-ring kit maintained at line side.
effectiveness_check: Zero fill volume OOS events on affected line for 6 months. PM compliance rate 100% on quarterly audit.

incident_summary: Particle size of HPMC lot LOT-34387 from API Solutions outside specification — D90 286.0µm against NMT 200µm. Discovered during incoming QC. Previous lots all compliant.
root_cause_detail: Supplier milling equipment calibration had drifted — milling screen replaced without re-qualification of particle size output. Change not notified to pharmaceutical customer.
corrective_action: Lot rejected. Supplier formally notified. All batches manufactured with this lot quarantined — dissolution testing performed. {random.randint(2,4)} batches showed dissolution impact and were rejected.
preventive_action: Supplier quality agreement updated — any equipment change affecting particle size requires 30-day prior notification and sample submission for qualification testing before shipment.
effectiveness_check: Zero particle size OOS results from this supplier for 24 months. Annual supplier process capability review for critical quality attributes.

incident_summary: Deviation report for batch B3031C closed without verification that all assigned corrective actions were completed. Two actions out of five remained open at time of closure.
root_cause_detail: QA reviewer closed deviation based on progress report from action owners without independently verifying completion. Closure criteria checklist not mandatory in previous QMS version.
corrective_action: Deviation reopened. Remaining two actions completed and independently verified by QA. Deviation re-closed with full evidence package attached.
preventive_action: QMS updated — deviation cannot be closed unless all linked corrective actions show 'Verified Complete' status in system. Verification requires QA sign-off separate from action owner sign-off.
effectiveness_check: Zero premature deviation closures for 12 months on monthly QA deviation audit.

incident_summary: Two raw material lots LOT-42892 and LOT-55188 mixed during dispensing for batch B9692A — both materials look identical. Mix-up detected by second operator during verification before batch manufacture began.
root_cause_detail: Both lots stored adjacent to each other on same shelf. Labels had similar colouring. Operator picked from wrong position under dim lighting conditions in dispensary.
corrective_action: Mixed quantities discarded. New quantities dispensed from correct identified lots. Dispensary lighting levels measured and found below recommended 500 lux — emergency lighting upgrade initiated.
preventive_action: Dispensary storage reorganised — similar-looking materials stored in separate zones with physical dividers. Lighting upgraded to 750 lux. Barcode scan of material and lot mandatory before dispensing.
effectiveness_check: Zero material mix-up events for 12 months. Dispensary lighting compliance ≥500 lux on quarterly environmental check.

incident_summary: Balance AC-790 failed calibration check before dispensing for batch D1196C. Drift of 0.37g recorded — tolerance ±0.01g. Previous calibration 5 weeks prior — due date missed.
root_cause_detail: Calibration due date not tracked in equipment management system. Balance due dates maintained manually in a spreadsheet — entry not updated after last calibration.
corrective_action: Balance taken out of service. All quantities dispensed for batch re-weighed on calibrated balance. No weight discrepancies found — batch released.
preventive_action: All balance calibration due dates entered into LIMS with auto-alert 7 days before due. No dispensing activity permitted on balance with overdue calibration.
effectiveness_check: 100% on-time calibration for all balances for 12 months on monthly compliance report.

root_cause_detail: Analyst misread SOP — volumetric flask size specified was 100mL but analyst used 250mL. Calculation error not identified before solution use. Peer review is advisory, not mandatory.

preventive_action: Redundant HVAC fan installed in Grade B suite. HVAC alarms mirrored to production area panel and QA office. Response procedure added to area emergency response document.
effectiveness_check: Zero HVAC differential pressure failures for 12 months. Alarm response time ≤15 minutes on monthly alarm log.

incident_summary: Coating pan GR-278 experienced nozzle blockage during batch A2844A film coating. Spray rate dropped causing uneven coat weight — 23.1% of tablets outside ±5.3% uniformity limit.
root_cause_detail: Nozzle orifice blocked by dried coating solution due to incorrect solution viscosity.
corrective_action: Coating halted. Nozzle removed, ultrasonically cleaned, and flow rate verified. Batch quarantined and reworked after QA approval. Equipment FD-379 re-validated before return to service.
preventive_action: Nozzle purge step added to start-up and end-of-run checklist. Nozzle inspection added to weekly PM schedule. Coating solution viscosity specification tightened with incoming test mandatory.
effectiveness_check: Zero coating OOS events over next 10 batches on this equipment. Nozzle PM compliance ≥ 100% for 3 months.

incident_summary: Sterility failure for batch D3867A — Staphylococcus epidermidis detected in 2/20 samples. Batch A5601C (2153 units) quarantined.
root_cause_detail: Gowning breach identified via CCTV review — operator touched non-sterile surface and continued filling without re-gloving. No supervisor present in fill suite at time of breach.
corrective_action: Batch recalled and destroyed. Filling line decontaminated and re-qualified. Operator suspended pending investigation. Supervisor accountability review initiated.
preventive_action: Continuous CCTV monitoring of fill suite with real-time QA review station. Supervisor presence in fill suite mandatory for first 30 minutes of each fill campaign. Re-gloving stations installed at 3 fill positions.
effectiveness_check: Zero sterility failures for 18 months. 100% gowning compliance on weekly CCTV audit.

incident_summary: Calibration certificate for FD-819 expired 67 days before equipment use during batch B1774B manufacture. Identified by QA during batch record review.
root_cause_detail: Calibration due dates maintained in manual spreadsheet — spreadsheet not updated when calibration was rescheduled by 3 weeks. No automated alert system.
corrective_action: Equipment taken out of service and calibrated. All results generated with equipment during out-of-calibration period reviewed. Batch data confirmed valid — no impact identified.
preventive_action: All equipment calibration due dates entered in LIMS with automated alert 30 and 14 days before due date. EBR prevents equipment use if calibration status is overdue.
effectiveness_check: 100% on-time equipment calibration for 12 months on LIMS compliance report.

incident_summary: Ambient temperature in dispensary C rose to 32.1°C for 3.8 hours due to air conditioning failure. Temperature-sensitive materials for batch D7096B dispensed during exceedance.
root_cause_detail: AC unit capacitor failed — no alarm configured for dispensary temperature. Exceedance discovered during routine operator check {round(random.uniform(2,5),1)} hours after onset.
corrective_action: Dispensed materials quarantined and tested for conformance against stability data. AC unit repaired. Materials released after QA review of stability data.
preventive_action: Continuous temperature monitoring with alarm to QA office and engineering installed in all dispensary areas. AC unit added to monthly PM schedule. Backup portable AC unit available for emergency deployment.
effectiveness_check: Dispensary temperature within 15–25°C for 12 months on continuous monitoring. Zero undetected temperature exceedances.

incident_summary: Wrong batch number entered in electronic batch record for batch C6876C by operator OP-8644. Error discovered during QA batch record review — two batches had same number in system.
root_cause_detail: Operator manually typed batch number rather than scanning barcode from manufacturing order. Keyboard transposition created duplicate batch number. EBR did not flag the duplicate.
corrective_action: Batch record corrected with full audit trail. Investigation confirmed no product mix-up occurred. System vendor engaged to add duplicate batch number validation.
preventive_action: EBR updated to reject duplicate batch numbers at entry — system alert raised immediately. Batch number entry by keyboard disabled — scan only via barcode reader.
effectiveness_check: Zero duplicate batch number events in EBR for 12 months.

incident_summary: Filling pump FP-944 o-ring failure caused variable fill volume on vial filling line 4 during batch A7864A. Fill weights ranged 0.92mL to 1.12mL against target 1.00mL ± 2%.
root_cause_detail: O-ring reached end of service life. PM interval of 6 months was insufficient for this filling speed. O-ring wear not inspected between PM events.
corrective_action: Filling halted. O-ring replaced. 100% gravimetric IPC check repeated across batch. Batch accepted after QA review of all fill weight data.
preventive_action: PM interval reduced from 6 to 3 months for all filling pump o-rings. O-ring inspection added to daily line clearance. Spare o-ring kit maintained at line side.
effectiveness_check: Zero fill volume OOS events on affected line for 6 months. PM compliance rate 100% on quarterly audit.

incident_summary: Stability sample for batch D9570C not collected at 12-month timepoint. Missing collection discovered 33 days after due date during stability manager review.
root_cause_detail: Stability schedule transferred manually to QC calendar on product changeover. Transfer error omitted this specific batch. No automated reminder system in place.
corrective_action: Accelerated stability assessment performed on retained samples. Bracketing and matrixing approach justified the missing timepoint per ICH Q1D. Regulatory authority notified per reporting requirements.
preventive_action: Stability schedules auto-generated and uploaded to LIMS on batch release. QC analyst receives LIMS reminder 7 and 3 days before each pull date. Missed timepoints auto-escalate to QA within 24 hours of due date.
effectiveness_check: Zero missed stability timepoints for 24 months. Monthly LIMS stability compliance report reviewed at QA management meeting.

incident_summary: HPLC instrument GR-247 column degradation produced OOS results for batch A6022A potency assay. Column pressure exceeded 507 bar during analysis run.
root_cause_detail: Column exceeded maximum injection count of 200. Column lifetime tracking not implemented — column replaced only on failure, not proactively.
corrective_action: Column replaced with new qualified column. All batch C4428C results invalidated and repeated using new column. System suitability confirmed before repeat analysis.
preventive_action: Column injection counter log implemented in LIMS. Mandatory replacement at 180 injections (90% of maximum). Column inventory managed with auto-alert at reorder point.
effectiveness_check: No column-related OOS events for 6 months post-implementation. 100% column injection count compliance on monthly audit.

incident_summary: Tablet press GR-221 punch wear resulted in weight variation OOS during compression of batch D6857C. RSD 4.6% against limit 2%. 24% of tablets outside ±5% weight limit.
root_cause_detail: Punches worn beyond allowable limit. No intermediate wear measurement performed between batches. Wear only detected at batch failure.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Worn punches replaced. Batch 100% weight-sorted; OOS tablets rejected. Replacement punches qualified before restart.
preventive_action: Punch wear gauge check added to start-up procedure and every 2 hours mid-run. Punch replacement triggered at 80% of maximum wear limit.
effectiveness_check: Zero punch-wear-related OOS events on this press for 12 months. Punch inspection records reviewed on monthly QA audit.

incident_summary: Temperature excursion in cold room CR-04 — reached 12.6°C for 1.2 hours. Limit 2–8°C. Batch D7287C and 8 other product batches potentially affected.
root_cause_detail: Refrigeration compressor failed overnight. Night shift operator did not respond to alarm within required 15 minutes — response was {round(random.uniform(45,120),0)} minutes after initial alarm.
corrective_action: Compressor replaced. All affected batches quarantined and thermal stability assessments initiated per product stability data. Engineering root cause investigation completed.
preventive_action: 24/7 alarm escalation: unacknowledged alarm after 10 minutes auto-escalates to on-call engineer via SMS. Backup refrigeration unit installed in critical cold rooms. Alarm response training refreshed for all shifts.
effectiveness_check: Zero temperature excursions exceeding 30 minutes for 12 months. 100% alarm response within 15 minutes on monthly alarm log review.

incident_summary: Dissolution failure at 30 minute timepoint for batch B2540B — 58.0% dissolved against NLT 80%. Three consecutive batches failed same timepoint.
root_cause_detail: Disintegrant particle size from new supplier lot was outside historical range. Smaller particle size reduced disintegrant performance. Incoming test for particle size was not performed on this excipient.
corrective_action: Three batches rejected. Emergency particle size testing of all remaining disintegrant stock. Alternative approved supplier lot sourced. Reformulation study initiated.
preventive_action: Particle size testing added to incoming QC specification for disintegrant. Supplier change notification procedure tightened — any new supplier lot requires comparative dissolution testing before use.
effectiveness_check: Zero dissolution failures for 12 months. 100% incoming particle size compliance for disintegrant on monthly QC report.

incident_summary: Temperature monitoring software failed to generate alarm for cold room exceedance of 10.4°C. Exceedance lasted 2.3 hours before discovered by operator. Batch C9083A affected.
root_cause_detail: Alarm suppression feature activated by engineer during sensor replacement — suppression not deactivated after work completed. No confirmation step in alarm management procedure.
corrective_action: Alarm suppression deactivated. Alarm system tested and verified functional. Affected batch quarantined and thermal assessment performed.
preventive_action: Alarm suppression requires dual sign-off (engineer + QA) with mandatory deactivation step on work completion form. Active suppressions visible on alarm dashboard — daily check mandated.

incident_summary: In-process pH of batch D1336C measured 4.0 — below validated range lower limit of 5.3 during granulation. Batch held pending investigation.
root_cause_detail: Purified water used for granulation fluid had transient low pH of 5.1 due to CO2 absorption from environmental exposure after storage tank vent filter change.
corrective_action: Batch rejected. Water system investigated — vent filter replaced and water re-qualified. pH specification for WFI tightened. Batch manufacturing process fully reviewed.
preventive_action: In-line pH monitoring of WFI at point of use — alert at pH 5.8, process stop at pH 5.5. Vent filter change now triggers WFI re-qualification before manufacturing use.
effectiveness_check: WFI pH within specification for 12 months on daily monitoring log. Zero pH-related granulation failures.

incident_summary: Visible particulate found in 32 vials during 100% visual inspection of batch A1395A (3707 total vials). Particles identified as glass lamellae from vial inner surface.
root_cause_detail: Glass vial supplier had process change — new vial mould introduced without notifying pharmaceutical customer. New mould produced higher rate of glass delamination under washing/sterilisation conditions.
corrective_action: Batch rejected and destroyed. All vials from same supplier lot quarantined. Supplier formally notified. Emergency supply sourced from qualified alternative supplier for ongoing production.
preventive_action: Supplier change notification clause added to vial supplier quality agreement — any process change requires prior notification and sample submission for qualification. Incoming vial delamination test introduced.
effectiveness_check: Zero glass particulate events for 12 months. Annual vial supplier audit to verify change notification process.

incident_summary: Grade A environmental monitoring exceeded action limit — 9 CFU detected at Fill Station #1 during batch B4316A. EU GMP Annex 1 limit: 0 CFU. Three consecutive positive samples.
root_cause_detail: HVAC pre-filter for LAF unit TBL-236 overdue for replacement by 55 days. Filter bypass reduced airflow velocity below 0.45 m/s — unidirectional airflow protection compromised.
corrective_action: Fill area taken out of service. Full decontamination with sporicidal agent. Filter replaced. Grade A re-qualified over 3 consecutive days. Batch quarantined for sterility testing.
preventive_action: HVAC PM integrated with production scheduling system — both systems display same PM calendar. Automated alert to Engineering Lead 14 and 7 days before filter due date. LAF velocity check added to daily line clearance.
effectiveness_check: Zero Grade A EM exceedances for 18 months. LAF velocity within specification on all daily checks.

incident_summary: Inventory management system showed 39kg more Magnesium Stearate than physically present — discrepancy discovered during quarterly stocktake for batch C4143B planning.
root_cause_detail: System rounding error in unit conversion (kg to g) accumulating over {random.randint(3,12)} months of transactions. Small per-transaction error undetected until stocktake.
corrective_action: Physical count performed. System corrected to match physical stock. Transaction history reviewed — rounding error traced to specific transaction type. Vendor engaged for fix.
preventive_action: Automatic monthly system-to-physical stock reconciliation for critical materials. Variance >1kg triggers immediate investigation. Rounding error fix deployed and validated.
effectiveness_check: Zero stock discrepancies >1kg for 12 months on monthly reconciliation report.

incident_summary: Analyst OP-9892 prepared HPLC standard solution at 77.0% of required concentration for batch B8580C assay. Results reported before error identified by second analyst during peer review.
root_cause_detail: Analyst misread SOP — volumetric flask size specified was 100mL but analyst used 250mL. Calculation error not identified before solution use. Peer review is advisory, not mandatory.
corrective_action: All results for batch invalidated. Standard solution remade at correct concentration. Analysis repeated by senior analyst. Correct results obtained — batch released.
preventive_action: Mandatory second-analyst verification of standard solution preparation before analysis begins. LIMS calculation check added — system flags if standard response factor deviates >5% from expected.
effectiveness_check: Zero standard preparation errors for 12 months. 100% peer review compliance on monthly QC audit.

incident_summary: QA analyst OP-7143 approved batch record for batch B7416B with 3 incomplete critical steps. Error discovered during post-release annual product review. Batch had been distributed.
root_cause_detail: QA reviewer working under time pressure at end of shift. Batch record review checklist used was outdated — 3 new mandatory steps added 2 months prior not on old checklist.
corrective_action: Batch recalled from distribution for risk assessment. Retrospective review of incomplete steps — all found to have been performed; documentation gap only. Batch rereleased after QA Director review.
preventive_action: QA batch record review checklist version-controlled in LIMS — old versions automatically retired on system when new version approved. Checklist update triggers mandatory retraining notification.
effectiveness_check: Zero incomplete batch record releases for 12 months. Monthly QA batch record audit: 100% checklist compliance.

incident_summary: Operator OP-2090 performed aseptic filling technique incorrectly during batch B5174B — contacted non-sterile surface then continued filling without re-gloving. Filling proceeded for 4.2 hours.
root_cause_detail: Operator's aseptic technique requalification was overdue by {days()} days. Requalification scheduling not tracked in training management system — overdue not flagged to supervisor.
corrective_action: Batch quarantined for sterility testing. Operator removed from aseptic operations. Full aseptic technique requalification completed including media fill participation.
preventive_action: Training due dates tracked in LIMS — overdue requalification automatically locks operator from aseptic filling in EBR. Supervisor receives 30-day advance notification of upcoming requalifications.
effectiveness_check: Zero aseptic technique-related contamination events for 18 months. 100% requalification on-time compliance on monthly training report.

incident_summary: Balance CEN-420 failed calibration check before dispensing for batch A3289A. Drift of 0.15g recorded — tolerance ±0.01g. Previous calibration 5 weeks prior — due date missed.
root_cause_detail: Calibration due date not tracked in equipment management system. Balance due dates maintained manually in a spreadsheet — entry not updated after last calibration.
corrective_action: Balance taken out of service. All quantities dispensed for batch re-weighed on calibrated balance. No weight discrepancies found — batch released.
preventive_action: All balance calibration due dates entered into LIMS with auto-alert 7 days before due. No dispensing activity permitted on balance with overdue calibration.
effectiveness_check: 100% on-time calibration for all balances for 12 months on monthly compliance report.

incident_summary: Moisture ingress in Microcrystalline Cellulose lot LOT-96546 — 6.3% moisture against NMT 2.0%. Packaging appeared intact. Discovered during incoming QC testing.
root_cause_detail: Supplier had temporary storage issue — materials stored in non-climate-controlled area during transit delay. Packaging moisture barrier integrity not tested before shipment.
corrective_action: Lot rejected. All in-house stock from same supplier lot quarantined and tested. {random.randint(2,5)} additional lots found non-compliant — all rejected.
preventive_action: Moisture indicator card added inside all excipient shipments — colour change on receipt triggers automatic rejection. Storage condition declaration required from supplier for all shipments.
effectiveness_check: Zero moisture OOS results on incoming testing for 12 months. 100% moisture indicator check compliance on incoming QC log.

incident_summary: Viable particle count in Grade B area exceeded action limit — 15 CFU/m³ at sample point 9 during mid of batch B3485A campaign.
root_cause_detail: Drain in Grade B area identified as microbial reservoir — drain cover cracked allowing backflow during cleaning. Drain had not been inspected for {days()} days.
corrective_action: Drain cover replaced and drain sanitised with sporicidal agent. Enhanced cleaning protocol implemented. Area re-tested — all results within specification before restart.
preventive_action: Drain inspection added to weekly environmental walkthrough checklist. Trend analysis for Grade B EM results implemented — 2 consecutive alerts trigger immediate drain inspection.
effectiveness_check: Zero Grade B EM action limit exceedances for 12 months. Weekly drain inspection compliance 100%.

incident_summary: QC analyst OP-8966 applied incorrect sampling plan to Microcrystalline Cellulose lot LOT-22541 incoming inspection — used Tier 2 plan for Tier 1 supplier. Under-sampled — missed non-conforming portion of lot.
root_cause_detail: Supplier tier assignment changed 2 months prior. Training on updated sampling procedure {sop()} had not been cascaded to all QC analysts — only 3 of 8 analysts had completed training.
corrective_action: Full re-sampling of lot performed using correct Tier 1 plan. Non-conforming portion identified and rejected. Remaining lot accepted after full testing. Training gap for all 8 analysts remediated immediately.
preventive_action: SOP updates triggering any change to sampling plans require mandatory training completion before effective date. SOP change cannot go live until training manager confirms 100% affected-staff completion.
effectiveness_check: Zero incorrect sampling plan application for 12 months. 100% SOP training completion before effective date on monthly training compliance report.

incident_summary: Two raw material lots LOT-68479 and LOT-37969 mixed during dispensing for batch B2644A — both materials look identical. Mix-up detected by second operator during verification before batch manufacture began.
root_cause_detail: Both lots stored adjacent to each other on same shelf. Labels had similar colouring. Operator picked from wrong position under dim lighting conditions in dispensary.
corrective_action: Mixed quantities discarded. New quantities dispensed from correct identified lots. Dispensary lighting levels measured and found below recommended 500 lux — emergency lighting upgrade initiated.
preventive_action: Dispensary storage reorganised — similar-looking materials stored in separate zones with physical dividers. Lighting upgraded to 750 lux. Barcode scan of material and lot mandatory before dispensing.
effectiveness_check: Zero material mix-up events for 12 months. Dispensary lighting compliance ≥500 lux on quarterly environmental check.

incident_summary: Calibration certificate for CEN-908 expired 72 days before equipment use during batch C6276A manufacture. Identified by QA during batch record review.

corrective_action: Equipment taken out of service and calibrated. All results generated with equipment during out-of-calibration period reviewed. Batch data confirmed valid — no impact identified.

effectiveness_check: 100% on-time equipment calibration for 12 months on LIMS compliance report.

incident_summary: Tablet hardness for batch B4570B failed specification — 65.0% of tablets below minimum 6.8 kP during IPC at 75% compression.
root_cause_detail: Compression force drifted below validated minimum — tablet press {equip()} main compression roll bearing showing early wear, causing force variation between strokes.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Bearing replaced. Batch 100% checked by automated hardness tester. Sub-specification tablets rejected. Remaining batch released after QA review.
preventive_action: Compression force monitoring added to real-time SCADA display with alert at ±5% from setpoint. Main compression roll bearing added to 6-monthly PM schedule.
effectiveness_check: Zero hardness OOS events on this press for 12 months. Bearing inspection records reviewed quarterly.

incident_summary: API lot LOT-60969 from ExciPure Corp failed assay — 93.4% against specification of 98.0–102.0%. Three retests confirmed OOS. Certificate of Analysis stated 99.1%.
root_cause_detail: Supplier analytical method was not equivalent to Ph. Eur. pharmacopoeial method used by the pharmaceutical manufacturer. Supplier method overestimated purity due to unresolved impurity peak.
corrective_action: Lot rejected and returned to supplier. Emergency stock from alternative qualified supplier sourced. Formal investigation requested from supplier within 30 days.
preventive_action: API supplier method comparability study mandated for all new lots — correlation between supplier and in-house methods must be ≥99% before lot acceptance. Annual method comparability audit introduced.
effectiveness_check: Zero API assay OOS results from supplier method discrepancy for 24 months. Annual method comparability confirmed on supplier audit report.

incident_summary: Incorrect cleaning agent (IPA 60%) applied to equipment CEN-473 by operator OP-1786 after batch B2474C manufacture. Approved agent is IPA 70%.
root_cause_detail: Cleaning agents stored side-by-side in unlabelled containers on cleaning trolley. Operator selected wrong bottle. Cleaning agent inventory not controlled at point of use.
corrective_action: Equipment re-cleaned with correct approved agent. Residue swab tested — passed before next manufacturing use. Incorrect agent containers removed from area immediately.
preventive_action: Cleaning agents dispensed from locked cabinet — key released only after correct agent confirmed on cleaning record. Containers colour-coded per equipment type. Cleaning SOP updated with photo guide.
effectiveness_check: Zero incorrect cleaning agent events for 12 months. Monthly cleaning audit: 100% correct agent usage verified.

incident_summary: Tablet press CEN-350 punch wear resulted in weight variation OOS during compression of batch B1268A. RSD 6.6% against limit 2%. 13% of tablets outside ±5% weight limit.
root_cause_detail: Punches worn beyond allowable limit. No intermediate wear measurement performed between batches. Wear only detected at batch failure.
corrective_action: Compression halted. Worn punches replaced. Batch 100% weight-sorted; OOS tablets rejected. Replacement punches qualified before restart.
preventive_action: Punch wear gauge check added to start-up procedure and every 2 hours mid-run. Punch replacement triggered at 80% of maximum wear limit.
effectiveness_check: Zero punch-wear-related OOS events on this press for 12 months. Punch inspection records reviewed on monthly QA audit.

incident_summary: Batch record for batch C7128A missing authorised signature at critical step 12 — fill start. Identified during QA batch record review before release.
root_cause_detail: Operator completed the step but was called away urgently before signing. Paper batch record was not locked — a different operator moved it, and the unsigned step was missed during QA review.
corrective_action: Batch record corrected with retrospective signature and deviation report. Investigation confirmed step was performed correctly. Batch released after QA Director review.
preventive_action: EBR system deployed for this product — paper batch records eliminated. EBR locks step until operator signs electronically in real time. Step cannot be bypassed.
effectiveness_check: Zero missing signatures in EBR for 12 months. 100% step sign-off compliance on monthly EBR audit.

incident_summary: Analyst OP-5668 prepared HPLC standard solution at 71.0% of required concentration for batch A8182B assay. Results reported before error identified by second analyst during peer review.

incident_summary: Foreign particulate contamination found in packaging material lot LOT-28868 — metal fragments found in 8.6% of units during incoming inspection. Fragments identified as stainless steel filings.
